description: Japanese author
prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Nagano Prefecture (長野県, Nagano-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [naꜜɡano, naɡanoꜜkeɴ]) is a landlocked prefecture of Japan located in the Chūbu region of Honshu.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Meiji, the romanization of the Japanese characters 明治, may refer to:

description: the most powerful and important noble clan in the Japanese history
description_wiki: Minamoto (源; Japanese pronunciation: [mʲi.na.mo.to]) was a noble surname bestowed by the Emperors of Japan upon members of the imperial family who were excluded from the line of succession and demoted into the ranks of the nobility since 814.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: Japanese Buddhist custom
description_wiki: Obon (お盆; [o.boꜜɴ]) or just Bon (盆; [boꜜɴ]) is a fusion of the ancient Japanese belief in ancestral spirits and a Japanese Buddhist custom to honor the spirits of one's ancestors.

description: national university of Japan
description_wiki: Nagoya University (名古屋大学, Nagoya daigaku), abbreviated to Meidai (名大) or NU, is a Japanese national research university located in Chikusa-ku, Nagoya.

surface_forms: fourth year of the Taisho period
description: period of Japanese history from 1912 to 1926, under the reign of Emperor Taishō
a polytheistic and animistic religion originating in Japan
description_wiki: Shinto (神道, Shintō; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜn.toː]), also called Shintoism, is the native religion and former ethnic cult of Japan.

description: Japanese new religion of Shinto origin
description_wiki: Tenrikyo (天 理 教, Tenrikyō) is a Japanese new religion which is neither strictly monotheistic nor pantheistic, originating from the teachings of a 19th-century woman named Nakayama Miki, known to her followers as "Oyasama".

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社, Suwa Jinja) may refer to:

description: collection of sacred books in Judaism and Christianity
description_wiki: The Bible is a collection of religious texts that are central to Christianity and Judaism, and esteemed in other Abrahamic religions such as Islam.

description: book of the New Testament
description_wiki: The Gospel of Matthew is the first book of the New Testament and one of the three synoptic Gospels.

description: group of samurai during the Boshin War
description_wiki: The Byakkotai (白虎隊; "White Tiger Unit") was a group of around 305 young teenage samurai of the Aizu Domain, who fought in the Boshin War (1868–1869) on the side of the Tokugawa shogunate.

description: noble family
description_wiki: The Tokugawa clan (徳川氏, Tokugawa-shi, Tokugawa-uji; Japanese pronunciation: [to.kɯꜜ.ɡa.wa, -ŋa.wa, -kɯ.ɡa.waꜜ.ɕi, -ŋa.waꜜ-]) is a Japanese samurai clan and dynasty which produced the Tokugawa shoguns who ruled Japan from 1603 to 1868 during the Edo period.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity
prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Nagano Prefecture (長野県, Nagano-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [naꜜɡano, naɡanoꜜkeɴ]) is a landlocked prefecture of Japan located in the Chūbu region of Honshu.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Gifu Prefecture (岐阜県, Gifu-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɡʲi.ɸɯ, ɡʲi.ɸɯ̥ꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūbu region of Honshu.

description: river flows thorough Chubu region of Japan
description_wiki: The Kiso River (木曽川, Kiso-gawa) is a river in the Chubu region of Japan roughly 229 km (142 mi) long, flowing through the prefectures of Nagano, Gifu, Aichi, and Mie before emptying into Ise Bay a short distance away from the city of Nagoya.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded on the teachings, person and life of Jesus
description_wiki: Christianity is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion, which states that Jesus is the Son of God and rose from the dead after his crucifixion, whose coming as the messiah (Christ) was prophesied in the Old Testament and chronicled in the New Testament.

description: dissolved municipality in Nishikasugai district, Aichi prefecture, Japan

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: school of Mahāyāna Buddhist Tradition
description_wiki: Zen (Japanese pronunciation: [dzeꜜɴ, dzeɴ]; from Chinese: Chan; in Korean: Sŏn, and Vietnamese: Thiền) is a Mahayana Buddhist tradition that developed in China during the Tang dynasty by blending Indian Mahayana Buddhism, particularly Yogacara and Madhyamaka philosophies, with Chinese Taoist thought, especially Neo-Daoist.

aliases: the president of the United States
President of the United States of America
description: head of state and head of government of the United States of America
description_wiki: The president of the United States (POTUS) is the head of state and head of government of the United States.

description: head of state of Japan
description_wiki: The emperor of Japan is the hereditary monarch and head of state of Japan.

entity: Coronation of the Emperor of Japan

description: geographic region in Northeast Asia
description_wiki: Manchuria is a historical region in Northeast Asia encompassing the entirety of present-day Northeast China and parts of the modern-day Russian Far East south of the Uda River and the Tukuringra-Dzhagdy ranges.

description: private university in Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Aoyama Gakuin University (Japanese: 青山学院大学, Hepburn: Aoyama Gakuin Daigaku; AGU) is a private Christian university in Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Yamanashi Prefecture (山梨県, Yamanashi-ken) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūbu region of Honshu.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: private boys' Junior and Senior High School in Minato, Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Azabu Junior and Senior High School (麻布中学校・高等学校, Azabu Chūgakkō Kōtōgakkō), often referred to simply as "Azabu", is a private preparatory day school in Japan.
